Imports

ast, genotype, spec

Types

Program[T] = ref ProgramObj[T]
Source   Edit   
ProgramFlag = enum
  FinestKnown, Cached, DeadCode
Source   Edit  

Consts

programCache = false
Source   Edit  

Procs

proc `$`(p: Program): string
renders the program as source code if possible; else raw ast Source   Edit   
proc `<`[T](a, b: Program[T]): bool
some objective measurement of two programs; score Source   Edit   
proc `<=`[T](a, b: Program[T]): bool
some objective measurement of two programs; score Source   Edit   
proc `==`[T](a, b: Program[T]): bool
some objective measurement of two programs; score Source   Edit   
proc addScoreToCache(p: Program; h: Hash; s: Option[Score]) {....deprecated.}
Deprecated
record the score for a given input hash Source   Edit   
proc cacheSize(p: Program): int {....deprecated.}
Deprecated
the size of a program's score cache Source   Edit   
proc clone[T](p: Program[T]): Program[T]
it's not a clone if it's different Source   Edit   
proc genome(p: Program): Genome {.inline.}
the program's source genome Source   Edit   
proc getScoreFromCache(p: Program; h: Hash): Option[Score] {....deprecated.}
Deprecated
attempt to retrieve the cached score for a given hash of the input Source   Edit   
proc isValid(p: Program): bool
true if the program is known to yield valid output; this will raise a defect if we have not scored the program yet Source   Edit   
func len(p: Program): int
some objective measurement of the program; ast length Source   Edit   
proc newProgram[T](a: Ast[T]): Program[T]
instantiate a new program from the given ast Source   Edit   
proc newProgram[T](a: Ast[T]; geno: Genome): Program[T]
instantiate a new program from the given ast and genome Source   Edit   
proc push(p: Program; s: Score)
record a valid score for statistics purposes Source   Edit   
proc render(p: Program): string
Source   Edit   
proc zombie(p: Program): bool {.inline.}
true if the program is invalid and may only be used for genetic data Source   Edit   
proc zombie=(p: Program; b: bool)
mark a program as invalid; idempotent Source   Edit
